tool_name: Bolted Joint Tightening Torque, Preload & Von Mises Utilisation (VDI 2230)

inputs.name: Thread Friction Coefficient (μG)
inputs.hint: Dry steel: 0.14–0.18 | Lightly oiled: 0.10–0.12 | MoS2 lubricated: 0.04–0.08 | Zinc-plated dry: 0.12–0.18

inputs.name: Bearing Face Friction Coefficient (μK)
inputs.hint: Same surface condition as thread friction — typically equal to μG

inputs.name: Effective Bearing Diameter (dw)
inputs.hint: Hex bolt: dw ≈ 1.5×d | Socket head: dw ≈ 1.4×d | Washer used: washer OD

inputs.name: Target Preload Utilisation of Yield
inputs.hint: VDI 2230 standard: 90% for torque-controlled tightening | 100% for yield-controlled (TTY)

inputs.name: Tightening Factor (αA) — Scatter
inputs.hint: VDI 2230 Table A7 — accounts for preload scatter

formulas: d2_pitch_diam = nominal_diameter_mm - (0.649519 * thread_pitch_mm)   // [mm] Pitch diameter — ISO 724
d3_minor_diam = nominal_diameter_mm - (1.226869 * thread_pitch_mm)   // [mm] Minor (root) diameter — ISO 724
A_stress = (3.14159 / 4) * POWER((d2_pitch_diam + d3_minor_diam) / 2, 2)   // [mm²] Stress area — ISO 898-1
F_preload_max = (preload_utilisation_pct / 100) * yield_strength_mpa * A_stress   // [N] Maximum assembly preload
F_preload_min = F_preload_max / alpha_val   // [N] Minimum preload after scatter — VDI 2230
lead_angle_rad = 3.14159 * thread_pitch_mm / (3.14159 * d2_pitch_diam)   // [rad] Thread helix angle
friction_thread_eff = friction_thread_mu_g / (3.14159 * POWER(POWER(1, 0.5 - POWER(lead_angle_rad, 0.5)), 0.5))   // simplified effective thread friction
T_thread = F_preload_max * (d2_pitch_diam / 2) * ((thread_pitch_mm / (3.14159 * d2_pitch_diam)) + friction_thread_mu_g / 0.866)   // [N·mm] Thread torque component
T_bearing = F_preload_max * friction_bearing_mu_k * (bearing_diameter_dw_mm / 2)   // [N·mm] Bearing face torque component
T_tightening_Nmm = T_thread + T_bearing   // [N·mm] Total tightening torque
sigma_tensile = F_preload_max / A_stress   // [MPa] Tensile stress in shank
sigma_vonMises = SQRT(POWER(sigma_tensile, 2) + 3 * POWER(tau_torsion, 2))   // [MPa] Combined Von Mises stress — VDI 2230 Eq.(R9)
K_nut_factor = T_tightening_Nmm / (F_preload_max * nominal_diameter_mm)   // [—] K-factor (Nut factor) — torque verification

pitch_vs_diameter.error_msg: Thread pitch cannot equal or exceed nominal diameter — geometrically impossible for ISO metric threads.

bearing_diameter_minimum.error_msg: Bearing face diameter (dw) must be greater than nominal diameter. Typical: dw ≈ 1.4–1.7 × d.

preload_pct_range.error_msg: Preload utilisation > 95%: Torsional component will cause yielding during tightening. VDI 2230 maximum for torque wrench: 90%. Use yield-controlled (TTY) method if 100% is required.

yield_strength_grade.error_msg: Yield strength > 1400 MPa: Exceeds standard property class range (12.9 = 1080 MPa). Hydrogen embrittlement risk is significant above 1200 MPa. Verify material specification.

smart_warnings.message: BOLT YIELDS DURING ASSEMBLY: Combined Von Mises stress exceeds yield strength. Bolt will neck and lose preload or fracture during tightening. Actions: (1) Lubricate to reduce μG and μK, (2) Reduce preload utilisation target to 80%, (3) Use higher-grade bolt.
Near-yield tightening: Utilisation 90–100%. Any friction variation above nominal will cause bolt yield. Calibrated torque wrench mandatory. Re-verify after first thermal cycle.

High thread friction (μG > 0.16): Most of applied torque is consumed overcoming friction — actual preload achieved will be much lower than calculated. Use thread lubricant or anti-seize compound. Re-measure μ with torque-angle method.

High scatter factor (αA > 1.6): Actual preload may be only 60% of target. Joint may not achieve required clamp force. Switch to hydraulic tensioning (αA = 1.2) or angle-controlled tightening (αA = 1.05).

smart_warnings.condition: joint_type === 'Dynamic / Fatigue — vibration, alternating load' && preload_utilisation_pct < 80
smart_warnings.message: FATIGUE JOINT — INSUFFICIENT PRELOAD: Dynamic joints require high preload to prevent fretting and self-loosening (Junker effect). Preload below 80% Rp0.2 with vibration loading is a known failure mode. Use thread-locking compound or prevailing torque nut.

High K-factor (>0.25): Friction-dominated joint. Standard torque tables assume K≈0.20. Your actual preload from nameplate torque values may be 25–40% lower than expected. Re-calibrate using measured K-factor for this specific surface combination.
